Norman Conks.description: A large Catholic sectarian street gang active in Glasgow, Scotland, from the 1880s to the 1960s. Based in the Catholic area of Norman Street in Bridgeton, they were initially a penny mob and evolved into a larger, influential razor gang. They were often involved in street fighting with the Protestant Billy Boys but had friendlier relations with the Calton Tongs. The gang included young women.

39ers gang.description: The 39ers gang is a hybrid street gang formed in early 2010 in New Orleans, Louisiana, from the merger of the G-Strip gang (Gallier Street) and 3-N-G gang (Third and Galvez). Primarily African American, the gang controls drug trafficking, particularly heroin, in Central City and the 9th Ward. Federal prosecutors have linked the gang to over 45 murders, including the 2010 double homicide of rapper Magnolia Shorty. In 2017, ten members were convicted on racketeering and murder charges.

400 Mawozo.description: The 400 Mawozo is the largest gang in Haiti, based in Ganthier and Port-au-Prince suburbs. Crips.description: The Crips are a primarily African-American alliance of street gangs based in Los Angeles, California, founded in 1969 by Raymond Washington and Stanley Williams. They are one of the largest and most violent street gang associations in the United States, with an estimated 30,000 to 35,000 members in 2008. Members are involved in drug trafficking, murder, robbery, and other crimes, and have a long-standing rivalry with the Bloods.

The Westies.description: The Westies was an Irish criminal gang based in Blanchardstown, west Dublin, Ireland. 'Ndrina Mancuso.description: The Mancuso are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Limbadi and Nicotera, in the province of Vibo Valentia, Calabria. Considered the most influential clan in the province, they are involved in international cocaine trafficking, extortion, usury, public works contracts, and money laundering. They have alliances with other 'Ndrangheta clans, Cosa Nostra, Colombian FARC, and AUC, and operate in northern Italy, central Italy, and abroad, notably in Togo. Described as the financially most powerful clan in Europe.

descriptions: Hamas official claims alliance with North Korea.
edges.evidence_quote: North Korean arms were likely used during the 7 Oct assault on Israel. Ali Barakeh, a Hamas official living in Lebanon, claimed the two are allies.

descriptions: North Korea tried to sell weapons to Houthis.
edges.evidence_quote: In August 2018, Reuters reported that a confidential United Nations investigation had found the North Korean government had failed to discontinue its nuclear and missile delivery programs, and in conjunction, was "cooperating militarily with Syria" and was "trying to sell weapons to Yemen's Houthis".

descriptions: North Korea shipped weapons to Houthis via Iran in 2024.
edges.evidence_quote: In January 2024, South Korea's Yonhap News Agency reported that North Korea had evidently shipped weapons to Houthis via Iran, based on the writings in Hangul script that were found on missiles launched towards Israel.
